revision.comment: Mr. Stradivarius moved page [[Template:Lua talk]] to [[Template:Lua]]: changing so this can be used on /doc pages too, not just on talk pages
add ability to add a note after the default message
allow automatic categorisation for String, Math, and BaseConvert modules
[[Help:Reverting|Reverted]] edits by [[Special:Contributions/Fred Bauder|Fred Bauder]] ([[User talk:Fred Bauder|talk]]) to last version by Mr. Stradivarius
Adding "type = style" to match the color and conceptual similarity of [[Template:intricate]]
adding note about lua modules not being substitutable, and providing for a substitutable alternate template (default "<templatename> subst").
revert - you *can* substitute Lua modules - just use {{subst:#invoke:foo|bar}}
switch back to type=notice per Johnuniq's comment on the talk page
Protected Template:Lua: [[WP:High-risk templates|Highly visible template]] ([Edit=Allow only template editors and admins] (indefinite) [Move=Allow only template editors and admins] (indefinite))
Removed protection from "[[Template:Lua]]": protection conflict. was declined at RfPP by another
convert this to a smaller version as per [[User:TheDJ]]'s suggestion on the talk page
check for templates with no {{{1}}} parameter
If first parameter is specified, use colon for the text, otherwise use dot character.
Convert this to use [[Module:Lua banner]]. It somehow seems fitting that a Lua template should actually be in Lua.

revision.comment: add ability to add a note after the default message
allow automatic categorisation for String, Math, and BaseConvert modules
[[Help:Reverting|Reverted]] edits by [[Special:Contributions/Fred Bauder|Fred Bauder]] ([[User talk:Fred Bauder|talk]]) to last version by Mr. Stradivarius
Adding "type = style" to match the color and conceptual similarity of [[Template:intricate]]
adding note about lua modules not being substitutable, and providing for a substitutable alternate template (default "<templatename> subst").
switch back to type=notice per Johnuniq's comment on the talk page
Protected Template:Lua: [[WP:High-risk templates|Highly visible template]] ([Edit=Allow only template editors and admins] (indefinite) [Move=Allow only template editors and admins] (indefinite))
convert this to a smaller version as per [[User:TheDJ]]'s suggestion on the talk page
check for templates with no {{{1}}} parameter
If first parameter is specified, use colon for the text, otherwise use dot character.
Convert this to use [[Module:Lua banner]]. It somehow seems fitting that a Lua template should actually be in Lua.

revision.comment: add ability to add a note after the default message
allow automatic categorisation for String, Math, and BaseConvert modules
[[Help:Reverting|Reverted]] edits by [[Special:Contributions/Fred Bauder|Fred Bauder]] ([[User talk:Fred Bauder|talk]]) to last version by Mr. Stradivarius
Adding "type = style" to match the color and conceptual similarity of [[Template:intricate]]
adding note about lua modules not being substitutable, and providing for a substitutable alternate template (default "<templatename> subst").
switch back to type=notice per Johnuniq's comment on the talk page
convert this to a smaller version as per [[User:TheDJ]]'s suggestion on the talk page
check for templates with no {{{1}}} parameter
If first parameter is specified, use colon for the text, otherwise use dot character.
Convert this to use [[Module:Lua banner]]. It somehow seems fitting that a Lua template should actually be in Lua.

{{Lua|Module:Lua banner}}
{{documentation}}
<!-- Categories go on the /doc subpage and interwikis go on Wikidata. -->
</noinclude>
